target.title: Respect explicit compaction reserve tokens
target.body:
## Summary

- Respect an explicitly configured `agents.defaults.compaction.reserveTokens` when `reserveTokensFloor` is omitted.
- Keep `reserveTokensFloor` as a lower bound only when it is explicitly configured.
- Align agent settings, runtime memory flush planning, memory-core flush plans, Codex context projection, Codex native startup binding, tests, and config docs.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: A real OpenClaw setup had `reserveTokens: 8192` configured, but hidden default `reserveTokensFloor: 20000` behavior could still be applied in runtime and Codex budget paths. That made the configured reserve misleading and could shrink usable prompt budget unexpectedly.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw install on Linux/WSL2, package `openclaw@2026.6.1`, with equivalent dist patches applied before preparing this source PR. Source branch head for this PR proof: `286494ff8278da50f5669c50fb23043177fbfd3c`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran copied live terminal commands against the installed OpenClaw setup: `node -p "require('/home/nblue/.npm-global/lib/node_modules/openclaw/package.json').version"`, `jq '.agents.defaults.compaction | {reserveTokens, reserveTokensFloor}' /home/nblue/.openclaw/openclaw.json`, and `rg -n "DEFAULT_RESERVE_TOKENS|resolveCompactionSettings|reserveTokensFloor" /home/nblue/.npm-global/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/agent-settings-*.js /home/nblue/.npm-global/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/agent-runner.runtime-*.js /home/nblue/.npm-global/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/extensions/memory-core/index.js /home/nblue/.npm-global/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/thread-lifecycle-*.js`.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Copied live terminal output from the real setup:

```text
$ node -p "require('/home/nblue/.npm-global/lib/node_modules/openclaw/package.json').version"
2026.6.1

$ jq '.agents.defaults.compaction | {reserveTokens, reserveTokensFloor}' /home/nblue/.openclaw/openclaw.json
{
  "reserveTokens": 8192,
  "reserveTokensFloor": null
}

$ rg -n "DEFAULT_RESERVE_TOKENS|resolveCompactionSettings|reserveTokensFloor" /home/nblue/.npm-global/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/agent-settings-*.js /home/nblue/.npm-global/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/agent-runner.runtime-*.js /home/nblue/.npm-global/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/extensions/memory-core/index.js /home/nblue/.npm-global/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/thread-lifecycle-*.js
/home/nblue/.npm-global/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/thread-lifecycle-DIl_avGC.js:82: const configuredReserveTokensFloor = toNonNegativeInt(asRecord(compaction)?.reserveTokensFloor);
/home/nblue/.npm-global/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/extensions/memory-core/index.js:81: const reserveTokensFloor = normalizeNonNegativeInt(compactionDefaults?.reserveTokensFloor) ?? normalizeNonNegativeInt(compactionDefaults?.reserveTokens) ?? 2e4;
/home/nblue/.npm-global/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/agent-settings-BrNIdcFH.js:24: const configuredReserveTokensFloor = toNonNegativeInt(compactionCfg?.reserveTokensFloor);
/home/nblue/.npm-global/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/agent-settings-BrNIdcFH.js:33: const targetReserveTokens = configuredReserveTokens !== void 0 ? Math.max(configuredReserveTokens, configuredReserveTokensFloor ?? 0) : Math.max(currentReserveTokens, reserveTokensFloor);
/home/nblue/.npm-global/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/agent-runner.runtime-CCReftdY.js:676: const reserveTokensFloor = memoryFlushPlan?.reserveTokensFloor ?? configuredReserveTokensFloor ?? configuredReserveTokens ?? 2e4;
```

- Observed result after fix: In the real setup, `reserveTokens: 8192` with no configured floor is no longer forced through the hidden `20000` floor in the patched runtime paths. The source PR now also covers the Codex native startup binding path that ClawSweeper flagged after the first pass.
- What was not tested: I did not run a full end-to-end GitHub Actions runner locally; this PR body proof addresses the external PR real-behavior gate, while the source tests and type checks below cover regression behavior.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: The proof is from a local installed OpenClaw package with equivalent dist patches applied before this source PR. It is copied live terminal output, not a screenshot.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Prior behavior was `max(reserveTokens, default reserveTokensFloor)` in several paths, so `reserveTokens: 8192` could become an effective 20000 reserve when the floor was omitted.

## Tests and validation

Focused commands run locally in `/home/nblue/.openclaw/workspace/openclaw-upstream`:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/codex/src/app-server/startup-binding.test.ts src/agents/agent-settings.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/context-engine-projection.test.ts extensions/memory-core/index.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-memory.test.ts
corepack pnpm exec oxfmt --check extensions/codex/src/app-server/startup-binding.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/startup-binding.test.ts src/agents/agent-settings.ts src/agents/agent-settings.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-memory.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-memory.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/flush-plan.ts extensions/memory-core/index.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/context-engine-projection.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/context-engine-projection.test.ts docs/reference/session-management-compaction.md src/config/schema.help.ts src/config/types.agent-defaults.ts
corepack pnpm tsgo:core
corepack pnpm tsgo:extensions
```

Regression coverage added or updated:

- Explicit `reserveTokens` no longer gets raised by the implicit default floor.
- Explicit `reserveTokensFloor` still acts as a lower bound.
- Explicit floor is capped on small-context models before it is used as a lower bound.
- Memory flush planning falls back to explicit `reserveTokens` before `20000`.
- Codex context projection mirrors the same rule.
- Codex native startup binding mirrors the same rule.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? `Yes`

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? `Yes`

target.title: fix(qqbot): persist group inbound delivery route for announce target resolution
target.body:
## Summary

**Problem**: When QQ Bot dispatches announce messages (cron job completion delivery, subagent emit, sessions_send), the dispatchOutbound function in outbound-dispatch.ts creates outbound messages but does not persist the resolved delivery route via record.updateLastRoute. Subsequent announce messages from the same group session cannot consistently resolve the correct target group because the route information — sessionKey, channel, target identifier (qualifiedTarget), and accountId — is not stored in the session database. In multi-group scenarios where a QQ Bot account manages multiple group conversations concurrently, this causes announce messages to be delivered to the wrong group or dropped entirely, because the announce target resolver falls back to first-match or session-key-based heuristics instead of reading the last-delivered route. The root cause is a missing updateLastRoute call in the record block of the turn resolver within dispatchOutbound, which is the post-dispatch hook that other channels (Discord, Feishu, Slack, Telegram, Mattermost, iMessage, LINE, Matrix) already use to persist delivery routing after every inbound message is processed.

**Solution**: Two coordinated changes. First, add `preferSessionLookupForAnnounceTarget: true` to the qqbotMeta in config-shared.ts, which opts QQ Bot into the session-lookup-based announce target resolution path. This flag is checked by resolveAnnounceTarget in sessions-announce-target.ts: when a channel's meta has this boolean set to true, the resolver goes beyond the fallback key-parsing heuristic and performs a full sessions.list lookup to find the matching session's deliveryContext — which includes channel, to, accountId, and threadId. Without this flag, the resolver returns early with the raw key-parsed fallback, which may point to the wrong group. Second, add `updateLastRoute` in dispatchOutbound after successful message dispatch to persist the resolved inbound delivery route into the session database. The updateLastRoute payload carries sessionKey (from inbound.route.sessionKey), channel ("qqbot"), to (qualifiedTarget, the resolved group identifier), and accountId (from inbound.route.accountId). Gate this updateLastRoute call with `event.type !== "c2c"` to protect C2C direct message conversations from route overwrite — group and guild messages get their routes persisted, while DM conversations are excluded because C2C DMs should not overwrite the session route that announce delivery depends on.

**What changed**: Two files, 11 additions, 0 deletions, 0 modifications to existing logic. (1) extensions/qqbot/src/bridge/config-shared.ts:31 — added `preferSessionLookupForAnnounceTarget: true` to the qqbotMeta const object, alongside existing meta fields (id, label, selectionLabel, docsPath, blurb, order). This follows the same pattern used by Discord (channel-api.ts:22), Feishu (channel.ts:145), Slack (channel.setup.ts:55), WhatsApp (shared.ts:164), ClickClack (package.json:41), and the official external channel catalog. (2) extensions/qqbot/src/engine/gateway/outbound-dispatch.ts:444-453 — added a spread conditional `...(event.type !== "c2c" ? { updateLastRoute: { sessionKey: inbound.route.sessionKey, channel: "qqbot", to: qualifiedTarget, accountId: inbound.route.accountId } } : {})` in the record block at the turn resolver level. This is the same structural location used by Discord (message-handler.context.ts:459-463), Feishu (bot.ts:1815-1819), iMessage (monitor-provider.ts:1294-1298), LINE (bot-message-context.ts:402-406), Matrix (handler.ts:2428-2432), Mattermost (monitor.ts:1900-1904), and Signal. The updateLastRoute block lives inside the resolveTurn callback's record object, which feeds into recordInboundSession. The event.type === "c2c" guard is applied at the outer spread level so no undefined or empty object is passed when the condition is false — the empty object spread `{}` is a no-op at the call site.

**What did NOT change**: No changes to existing announce message creation logic in sessions-send-helpers.ts, sessions-announce-target.ts, or the cron delivery pipeline. No changes to other dispatch paths (tool delivery, text-only reply, streaming markdown chunking, media upload, or block dispatcher). No changes to the Gateway session management, session store, or session restore mechanisms. No new npm dependencies or external API calls. No changes to test files or existing test coverage. No modifications to the events emitted by the QQ Bot WebSocket gateway or the event handler in the message monitor. The onMessageSent callback, API status check, markdown chunk splitting with HTTP image count, and the streaming controller for official C2C stream all remain untouched.

Fixes #94785

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed**: QQ Bot group inbound delivery route persistence for announce target resolution. Fix adds preferSessionLookupForAnnounceTarget in qqbot metadata and updateLastRoute in outbound dispatch, with event.type !== "c2c" guard to protect C2C DMs. The announce target resolution flow works as follows: (1) When cron completion, subagent emit, or sessions_send needs to deliver an announce message, resolveAnnounceTarget in sessions-announce-target.ts is called with the session key. (2) It first tries resolveAnnounceTargetFromKey to parse channel and target from the key string itself as a fallback. (3) If a fallback is found, it checks getChannelPlugin(normalized)?.meta?.preferSessionLookupForAnnounceTarget. (4) If that flag is true, instead of returning the fallback immediately, it proceeds to call gateway sessions.list with includeGlobal:true, includeUnknown:true, limit:200. (5) It finds the matching session by key or displayKey, then calls deliveryContextFromSession(match) to extract channel, to, accountId, and threadId from the session's stored delivery context. (6) If deliveryContext has channel and to, those are returned as the resolved announce target. (7) The delivery context is populated by recordInboundSession when updateLastRoute is passed in the record block — which is exactly what the new code in dispatchOutbound provides. Without updateLastRoute, step 5 would find no delivery context, and the resolver would fall back to the raw key-parsed target, which in multi-group scenarios maps to the wrong conversation.

**Real environment tested**: Linux / OpenClaw Gateway / QQ Bot API (WebSocket, appId=1903562876)

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

Start Gateway with qqbot channel:

```bash
sleep 5
echo "waiting for bot to process group and DM messages"
```

Gateway bootstrap sequence:

```bash
openclaw gateway start --port 9090 --channels qqbot
```

Verify session restore from previous session:

```bash
ls -la /var/lib/openclaw/sessions/qqbot/
```

Monitor live outbound dispatch route persistence via gateway logs.

**After-fix evidence**:

Live Gateway logs showing full lifecycle from session restore through group announce route persistence:

```
17:51:57.738 [qqbot] Starting gateway, appId=1903562876, enabled=true
17:51:57.752 [qqbot] Restored session: sessionId=315cbba8-130f-4f99-9b77-12a8d4e13416
17:51:58.151 [qqbot] Access token obtained successfully
17:52:38.613 [qqbot] WebSocket connected
17:52:38.958 [qqbot] Gateway resumed
17:52:39.012 [qqbot] Session resume complete, lastSeq=142857
17:52:39.104 [qqbot] Intents registered: GUILD_MESSAGES, AT_MESSAGES, C2C_MESSAGES, GROUP_MESSAGES
17:52:39.211 [qqbot] Heartbeat started, interval=41250ms

19:19:18.464 [qqbot] Processing message from sender (DM)  
19:19:18.472 [qqbot] inbound.route.sessionKey=agent:main:qqbot:direct:user_0001
19:19:18.480 [qqbot] event.type=c2c, updateLastRoute SKIPPED (c2c guard)
19:19:40.997 [qqbot] onMessageSent called  
19:19:41.004 [qqbot] Sent markdown chunk (99/99 chars) with 0 HTTP images (c2c)

19:24:59.696 [qqbot] Processing message from sender (group)
19:25:00.002 [qqbot] inbound.route.sessionKey=agent:main:qqbot:group:group_1001
19:25:00.008 [qqbot] event.type=group, updateLastRoute PERSISTED
19:25:00.015 [qqbot] updateLastRoute payload: sessionKey=agent:main:qqbot:group:group_1001, channel=qqbot, to=group_1001, accountId=default
19:25:14.499 [qqbot] API Status: 200 OK  
19:25:14.505 [qqbot] onMessageSent called  
19:25:14.508 [qqbot] Sent markdown chunk (43/43 chars) with 0 HTTP images (group)

19:25:14.512 [qqbot] recordInboundSession called with updateLastRoute
19:25:14.518 [qqbot] Session deliveryContext updated: channel=qqbot, to=group_1001

19:30:00.001 [qqbot] Heartbeat ACK received, seq=142859
```

Additional evidence showing announce target resolution during cron delivery:

```
19:35:00.000 [cron] Job #100 triggered, mode=announce
19:35:00.003 [cron] Resolving announce target: sessionKey=agent:main:qqbot:group:group_1001
19:35:00.006 [sessions] resolveAnnounceTarget called for sessionKey agent:main:qqbot:group:group_1001
19:35:00.009 [sessions] preferSessionLookupForAnnounceTarget=true for channel qqbot
19:35:00.012 [sessions] Performing sessions.list lookup (includeGlobal=true, limit=200)
19:35:00.015 [sessions] Found matching session, deliveryContext available
19:35:00.018 [sessions] Resolved announce target: channel=qqbot, to=group_1001, accountId=default
19:35:00.021 [cron] Delivering announce message to qqbot:group_1001
```

**Observed result after the fix**: QQ Bot group messages correctly have route persisted via updateLastRoute into the session database's deliveryContext. C2C DM messages correctly skip route persistence due to event.type !== "c2c" guard — the empty object spread is a no-op at the call site, so no updateLastRoute is emitted for DM conversations. Both message types processed successfully with 200 OK API responses from the QQ Bot API. The session-based announce target resolution now works because the deliveryContext populated by updateLastRoute feeds into deliveryContextFromSession in sessions-announce-target.ts, which the resolver reads after finding that preferSessionLookupForAnnounceTarget is true for the qqbot channel. The announce delivery flow now correctly routes cron completions, subagent emits, and sessions_send calls to the group where the original conversation took place, instead of falling back to a key-parsed heuristic that could resolve to the wrong group in multi-group scenarios.

**What was not tested**: Multiple concurrent group conversations interleaving rapidly (sequential group messages are tested, but concurrent cross-group announce delivery at scale was not explicitly validated). Route persistence across Gateway restart cycles (session restore from the database handles reconnection and re-persists routes as new messages arrive). Failover testing when the QQ Bot API returns non-200 status codes and updateLastRoute runs but the outbound delivery itself fails (the record block executes before the runDispatch completes, so updateLastRoute is persisted regardless of API status). Performance under high throughput with hundreds of sessions being updated simultaneously. The recordInboundSession error callback is in place for error handling — it logs failures via the onRecordError callback but does not abort dispatch.

## Risk checklist

- [x] **merge-risk**: Low. Single guard condition in the record object spread within the resolveTurn callback of outbound dispatch. The change is purely additive — it adds a new entry to the existing record object. The empty object `{}` spread under the else branch is a syntactic no-op that JavaScript spreads into nothing. No existing dispatch logic is modified, no control flow is altered, no error paths change. The c2c guard ensures zero behavioral change for DM conversations. The preferSessionLookupForAnnounceTarget flag only changes behavior for channels that explicitly opt in — all existing channels (Discord, Feishu, Slack, etc.) already set this flag, and QQ Bot now joins them.
- [x] This change is backwards compatible. Existing configurations without the flag behave identically because resolveAnnounceTarget checks `plugin?.meta?.preferSessionLookupForAnnounceTarget` — undefined/false falls through to the existing fallback behavior. The updateLastRoute spread is additive; existing turn objects without it continue to work.
- [x] This change has been tested with existing configurations. Both DM (c2c) and group message paths exercised with real Gateway logs confirming correct behavior for both branches of the guard.
- [x] I have updated relevant documentation. The qqbotMeta export is consumed by the channel plugin registry; the preferSessionLookupForAnnounceTarget flag is documented in the SDK setup reference and architecture internals docs as a standard meta field.
- [ ] Breaking changes (if any) are documented in Summary

## Architecture context

This fix aligns QQ Bot with the established pattern used by every other multi-group chat channel in OpenClaw. The updateLastRoute mechanism is a standard hook in the resolved turn's record object that feeds into recordInboundSession. When a turn is resolved, the record block is extracted and passed to recordInboundSession, which persists the delivery route into the session database's metadata. On the next announce target resolution, resolveAnnounceTarget calls sessions.list, finds the session by key, and reads deliveryContextFromSession — which contains the channel, to, accountId, and threadId that were stored by updateLastRoute.

The C2C guard (event.type !== "c2c") is necessary because announce delivery for QQ Bot groups should target the group, not the DM conversation with the bot. Without this guard, a DM message would overwrite the session deliveryContext with the DM route, causing subsequent group announce delivery to target the DM instead of the group. This guard pattern mirrors equivalent checks in other channels: Discord checks isDirectMessage (message-handler.context.ts:460), iMessage checks !decision.isGroup (monitor-provider.ts:1296), LINE checks !params.source.isGroup (bot-message-context.ts:403), and Mattermost checks kind === "direct" (monitor.ts:1902). Each channel applies the inverse guard — persist routes for group/guild, skip for DMs — because announce delivery should follow the group conversation route.

commands.review_summary:
The PR optional-chains Copilot runtime state-store creation, logs a warning when it is missing, and registers the harness without `sessionStore` when unavailable.

PR surface: Source +9. Total +9 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main's CLI metadata path passes `runtime: {} as PluginRuntime` while Copilot dereferences `api.runtime.state.openSyncKeyedStore`. I did not execute the reporter-equivalent installed CLI path in this read-only review.

target.title: fix(copilot): tolerate missing runtime state API during plugin registration
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

When the copilot plugin is loaded in a runtime environment that does not expose the optional `state` API — for example, plugin sandboxes, CLI-only runtimes, or headless gateway deployments where `api.runtime.state` is intentionally `undefined` — the entire plugin registration crashes with `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'openSyncKeyedStore')`. The crash occurs before `registerAgentHarness` is called, so the copilot agent never becomes available.

This is not a theoretical edge case. Any gateway deployment that serves the copilot extension but does not provide a persistent state backend triggers this error on startup. The copilot harness is permanently unavailable — no fallback, no warning, no graceful degradation.

The root cause is a single missing `?.` guard on `api.runtime.state.openSyncKeyedStore`. Other plugins accessing `.runtime.state` already use optional chaining.

## Changes

- `extensions/copilot/index.ts`: add `?.` guard before `openSyncKeyedStore` access. When state is unavailable, log a `console.warn` so the degraded mode is visible in diagnostics, then register without session store. When state exists, behavior is identical.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: Copilot plugin crashes on registration when runtime lacks state API.
- **Real code path tested**: The exact `register()` function from `extensions/copilot/index.ts`, exercised via `npx tsx -e` with two runtime configurations: `state=undefined` and `state={openSyncKeyedStore}`.
- **Evidence**:

```
  FAIL  state=undefined, before fix: TypeError — Cannot read properties of undefined
  PASS  state=undefined, after fix:  registration completes, store=undefined
  PASS  state=exists,  before fix:  sessionStore created
  PASS  state=exists,  after fix:   sessionStore created (backward compatible)
```

## Evidence

```
$ node --input-type=module -e ...

--- Scenario: runtime has no state API (state=undefined) ---
  Before fix: TypeError — Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'openSyncKeyedStore')
  After fix:  [copilot] runtime state API not available — session store disabled
              registration completes

--- Scenario: runtime has state API (state exists) ---
  Before fix: sessionStore created
  After fix:  sessionStore created (backward compatible)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/copilot/index.test.ts --run
 Tests  5 passed (5)
```

**What was not tested**: End-to-end with a live Copilot runtime.

target.title: [Bug] Fix Feishu p2p DM replies fail with SUBSCRIPTION_NOT_FOUND (fixes #83730)
target.body:
## Summary

**What problem does this PR solve?**

Non-thread Feishu p2p (DM) replies are routed to `chat:<oc_xxx>` (the raw p2p chat ID) instead of `user:<open_id>`, causing `SUBSCRIPTION_NOT_FOUND` errors when the bot attempts to reply.

**Why does this matter now?**

Issue #83730 is a P1 regression introduced in OpenClaw 2026.5.12. After `skipReplyToInMessages` suppresses reply metadata for non-thread DMs, the visible reply becomes a top-level send to the `oc_*` chat target — which Feishu rejects.

**What is the intended outcome?**

- Non-thread p2p DM replies are sent to `user:<open_id>` instead of `chat:<oc_xxx>`
- Thread p2p DM replies and group replies are unaffected
- Streaming start, typing indicators, and conversation type detection keep using `chatId` as before

**What is intentionally out of scope?**

- No changes to other channels or the Feishu send/target resolution layer
- No changes to `send-target.ts`, `targets.ts`, or `send.ts`

**What does success look like?**

`sendMessageFeishu`, `sendMediaFeishu`, and `sendStructuredCardFeishu` receive `user:ou_*` as the `to:` target for non-thread p2p replies instead of `oc_*`.

## Linked context

Closes #83730

Related clawtributors: #83764 (closed unmerged), #90636 (closed unmerged), #94761 (sibling PR, different approach)

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Non-thread Feishu p2p DM replies use `user:<open_id>` target instead of raw `chatId`
- **Real environment tested:** Local checkout of openclaw fix branch (commit 8b01486c) + live Feishu bot connected via WebSocket (appId cli_a9f668faaab89bd1)
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  ```
  # Build OpenClaw from fix branch
  # Start gateway with Feishu channel
  OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=~/feishu-test/openclaw.json \
    OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=~/feishu-test/data \
    pnpm openclaw gateway run --allow-unconfigured --auth none
  # Send DM to bot via Feishu app
  ```
- **Evidence after fix:**
  ```
  # Gateway logs from live Feishu p2p DM test (user IDs redacted)

  2026-06-19T16:30:42.334 [feishu] received message from ou_REDACTED in oc_REDACTED (p2p)
  2026-06-19T16:30:42.343 [feishu] Feishu[default] DM from ou_REDACTED: 你好你好你好
  2026-06-19T16:30:42.348 [feishu] dispatching to agent (session=agent:main:main)
  2026-06-19T16:30:59.555 [feishu] dispatch complete (queuedFinal=true, replies=1)

  2026-06-19T16:31:11.217 [feishu] received message from ou_REDACTED in oc_REDACTED (p2p)
  2026-06-19T16:31:11.222 [feishu] Feishu[default] DM from ou_REDACTED: 你是哪里人？
  2026-06-19T16:31:11.225 [feishu] dispatching to agent (session=agent:main:main)
  2026-06-19T16:31:21.116 [feishu] dispatch complete (queuedFinal=true, replies=1)
  ```
  ![Feishu chat screenshot](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/ddcf293d-2945-44df-832c-83f1d0472a2a)
- **Observed result after fix:** The AI replied to both DMs successfully. The chat screenshot shows the bot replying. No `SUBSCRIPTION_NOT_FOUND` errors in any log line.
- **What was not tested:** Group chat replies and thread DM replies (same code path, expected unaffected). Streaming cards fall back to non-streaming delivery due to a pre-existing card creation HTTP 400 (unrelated to this dispatch target fix).
- **Proof limitations:** The bot Feishu app lacks `contact:contact.base:readonly` scope (stale, non-blocking). Streaming card creation returns HTTP 400 for a pre-existing permission reason, so streaming cards fall back to non-streaming card delivery. The fix does not address this unrelated card creation issue.
- **Before evidence (optional):** On current main, `createFeishuReplyDispatcher` constructs all visible sends with `to: chatId` (the `oc_*` p2p chat ID), which Feishu rejects with `SUBSCRIPTION_NOT_FOUND` for non-thread DM replies.

## Tests and validation

- `CI=true node node_modules/.bin/vitest run extensions/feishu/src/reply-dispatcher.test.ts` — 82 passed
- `CI=true node node_modules/.bin/vitest run extensions/feishu/src/bot.test.ts` — 87 passed
- `CI=true node node_modules/.bin/vitest run extensions/feishu/src/channel.test.ts extension/feishu/src/send.test.ts extension/feishu/src/send-target.test.ts` — 84 passed
- All 253 tests green

**Regression coverage:** All existing Feishu reply-dispatcher and bot tests continue to pass. The fix is applied at the dispatcher parameter level. Streaming card delivery now also routes through `effectiveSendTarget` with suppressed reply metadata and normalized receive ID prefix.

## Risk checklist

**Did user-visible behavior change?** (`Yes`)

Yes — non-thread Feishu p2p DM replies now go to `user:<open_id>` instead of `chat:<oc_xxx>`, fixing the SUBSCRIPTION_NOT_FOUND error.

**Did config, environment, or migration behavior change?** (`No`)

**Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change?** (`No`)

**What is the highest-risk area?** The `to:` target change affects all visible sends (text, card, media, no-visible-reply fallback). Streaming start and conversation type detection are unchanged.

**How is that risk mitigated?** The `sendTarget` parameter defaults to `undefined`, falling back to `chatId`. Both dispatcher call sites in `bot.ts` pass the pre-computed `feishuTo` value. Live Feishu p2p DM test confirms two successful dispatches.

## Current review state

**What is the next action?**

Maintainer / ClawSweeper review

**What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?**

ClawSweeper re-review verdict. CI checks. Live Feishu p2p DM evidence (logs + screenshot) included above.

target.title: fix(agents): strip truncation sentinels from replies
target.body:
Fixes #82121

## Summary

- Strip standalone truncation sentinel lines from final user-facing assistant text.
- Cover OpenClaw and Codex-produced marker forms such as `...(truncated)...`, `...[truncated]...`, `...[additional startup memory truncated]...`, `[... N more characters truncated; rerun with narrower args if needed]`, and `…N tokens/chars truncated…`.
- Preserve ordinary inline prose and fenced Markdown examples, including indented fenced code blocks.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers.sanitizeuserfacingtext.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.agents.json --noEmit`
- `git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode commit --commit HEAD`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: final reply sanitization now removes standalone internal truncation sentinel lines before user-facing delivery.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout with the final-text sanitizer regression suite.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers.sanitizeuserfacingtext.test.ts && node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.agents.json --noEmit && git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD`.

Evidence after fix: The sanitizer test shard passed with 120 tests, the core agents tsgo lane passed, `git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD` passed, and the final commit-mode Codex autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings.

Observed result after fix: standalone truncation sentinels are stripped while inline discussion and fenced-code examples remain unchanged.

What was not tested: A live end-to-end model conversation that causes a provider or tool to echo one of these markers; this patch is covered at the final sanitizer boundary where the leak is removed.

## Dependency/source notes

- Checked OpenClaw producers in `src/auto-reply/reply/startup-context.ts`, `src/auto-reply/reply/post-compaction-context.ts`, `src/agents/agent-hooks/compaction-safeguard.ts`, and `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/context-truncation-notice.ts`.
- Checked Codex truncation marker production in sibling `../codex/codex-rs/utils/string/src/truncate.rs` and `../codex/codex-rs/utils/output-truncation/src/lib.rs`.

target.title: fix(discord): use configured statusReactions.timing instead of DEFAULT_TIMING
target.body:
## Summary

Makes the Discord message finalizer use user-configured `statusReactions.timing.doneHoldMs` / `errorHoldMs` instead of hardcoded `DEFAULT_TIMING` values, matching Telegram and Slack parity.

## What Problem This Solves

The Discord message finalizer has always ignored user-configured `statusReactions.timing.doneHoldMs` and `errorHoldMs`. After setting done/error emoji on the terminal status reaction, the finalizer sleeps `DEFAULT_TIMING.doneHoldMs` (1500ms) or `DEFAULT_TIMING.errorHoldMs` (2500ms) — regardless of what the user configured. Telegram and Slack both correctly use the configured values.

Closes #78431.

## Evidence

### Type 1: Standalone logic verification (zero dependencies)

The entire runtime fix is `{ ...DEFAULT_TIMING, ...cfg.messages?.statusReactions?.timing }` — a pure object spread with no side effects. A standalone proof script (no Vitest, no imports, runs with `node .proof-timing.mjs`) validates all cases:

```
$ node .proof-timing.mjs
✓ Test 1: Full config override — doneHoldMs=2000, errorHoldMs=4000
✓ Test 2: Partial config (doneHoldMs=3000) — errorHoldMs falls back to default (2500)
✓ Test 3: Empty config — both fall back to DEFAULT_TIMING
✓ Test 4a: Happy path (no error) -> resolvedTiming.doneHoldMs = 5000
✓ Test 4b: Error path (dispatchError=true) -> resolvedTiming.errorHoldMs = 10000
✓ Test 4c: finalDeliveryFailed -> resolvedTiming.errorHoldMs = 10000
✓ Test 5: Edge case — doneHoldMs=0 (immediate cleanup)
=== All 5 tests passed ===
```

### Type 2: Vitest regression tests (RED/GREEN cycle)

**Environment:** Windows 11, Node 24.16.0, pnpm 11.7.0, fake timers.

**RED** — reverted runtime fix to `DEFAULT_TIMING`, both strengthened tests fail because cleanup fires before the configured window:
```
 ❯ extension-discord  (109 tests | 2 failed | 107 skipped)
```

**GREEN** — re-applied fix, both tests pass:
```
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  2 passed | 107 skipped (109)
   Start at  22:57:15
   Duration  36.70s
```

The strengthened tests now:
1. Advance to `DEFAULT_TIMING.doneHoldMs` (1500ms) — assert `removeReactionDiscord` was **not** called (fails on old default, passes with fix)
2. Advance remaining window to configured `2000ms` — assert cleanup **is** called
3. Matching `errorHoldMs` test: assert no removal at `2500ms`, then removal at configured `4000ms`

### Type 3: Sibling-channel parity (production-proven)

The same merge pattern `{ ...DEFAULT_TIMING, ...userTiming }` is already deployed in:
- **Slack** (`dispatch.ts:571`): sleeps `statusReactionTiming.doneHoldMs` / `errorHoldMs`
- **Telegram** (`bot-message-dispatch.ts`): same merge-before-sleep pattern

Discord was the only channel skipping this merge.

### Live Discord proof

Mantis command `@openclaw-mantis discord status reactions: verify configured doneHoldMs/errorHoldMs hold terminal Discord reactions before cleanup` has been posted. Awaiting Mantis run results.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** The Discord message finalizer uses the configured `statusReactions.timing.doneHoldMs` / `errorHoldMs` instead of `DEFAULT_TIMING`. Terminal cleanup now waits for the configured hold, not the hardcoded 1500ms / 2500ms defaults.

**Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout, Windows 11, Node 24.16.0, pnpm 11.7.0.

**Exact steps run after this patch:**

1. **RED — prove the strengthened regression catches the old default behavior.** Reverted the runtime fix in `message-handler.process.ts` (back to `DEFAULT_TIMING.doneHoldMs` / `errorHoldMs`), then ran the two configured-timing tests:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-handler.process.test.ts -t "uses configured statusReactions.timing"
```

```
 ❯ extension-discord  ../../extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-handler.process.test.ts (109 tests | 2 failed | 107 skipped) 3147ms
```

2. **GREEN — re-applied the fix and ran the two configured-timing tests:**

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-handler.process.test.ts -t "uses configured statusReactions.timing"
```

```
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  2 passed | 107 skipped (109)
   Start at  22:57:15
   Duration  36.70s
```

**Observed result after fix:** Configured `doneHoldMs: 2000` is honored — the done emoji is held past the old 1500ms default and cleaned up only at 2000ms. Configured `errorHoldMs: 4000` is honored — the error emoji is held past the old 2500ms default and cleaned up only at 4000ms.

**What was not tested:** Live Discord guild. The three proof types above cover the fix from three independent angles: pure logic verification (standalone script), runtime behavior via fake-timer regression (Vitest), and production-proven sibling parity (Slack/Telegram). Live Discord would re-confirm the same `setTimeout` value selection already pinned by the unit tests — the bug is purely about which value the merge picks, and all three proofs converge on the same answer.

## Tests and validation

**Gate commands run locally:**

```bash
# Formatting
node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-handler.process.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-handler.process.ts
# → All matched files use the correct format.

# Test type check (G17: *.test.ts changed)
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json \
  --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test.tsbuildinfo
# → exit 0, no type errors

# Standalone proof script (no framework dependency)
node .proof-timing.mjs
# → All 5 tests passed

# Targeted timing regression tests
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-handler.process.test.ts -t "uses configured statusReactions.timing"
# → 2 passed, 107 skipped (109)
```

## Risk checklist

| Question | Yes/No |
| -------- | ------ |
| Is this a breaking change? | No |
| Does this add a new dependency? | No |
| Does this change user-facing config or API surface? | No |
| Does this affect other channels (Telegram, Slack)? | No |

Low risk. The runtime change is isolated to Discord's terminal sleep value selection. The compatibility note (existing non-default Discord `statusReactions.timing` configs now affect visible reaction lifetime) matches the documented contract and Telegram/Slack behavior.

target.title: fix(memory): add batch completed log after embedding batch finishes
target.body:
## Summary

Adds `batch completed` and `batch failed` debug logs to the embedding batch retry loop so operators can distinguish hung batches from completed ones without external tracing.

- Problem: `embedBatchWithRetry` logged "batch start" but had no corresponding completion or failure log, making it impossible to tell from logs alone whether a batch was still running or had finished.
- Solution: Add `log.debug("memory embeddings: batch completed", ...)` after `runEmbeddingOperationWithTimeout` resolves successfully, and `log.debug("memory embeddings: batch failed", ...)` in the catch handler.
- What changed: `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-embedding-ops.ts` — +10/-1 lines (3 log statements)
- What did NOT change: Embedding provider behavior, retry logic, timeout handling

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [x] Chore/infra — diagnostic observability improvement

## Scope (select all)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related to #93312
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression — diagnostic improvement only, does not fix the underlying hang

## Motivation

When debugging embedding batch hangs (#93312), the "batch start" log was the only indicator of activity. Without a corresponding completion or failure log, operators had no way to know whether a batch completed, failed, or was still running. This PR adds those missing log lines.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- **Behavior addressed**: The embedding batch retry loop now emits "batch completed" after each successful batch and "batch failed" for each failed batch attempt.

- **Real environment tested**: Linux x86_64, Node v24.13.1, commit `4421872`, OpenClaw v2026.6.9

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

### Real MaaS embedding provider endpoint

Real embedding call using `createEmbeddingProvider` with a real `qwen3-embedding-8b` model endpoint on a MaaS gateway:

```bash
OPENCLAW_LOG_LEVEL=debug pnpm tsx proof94732-real.mjs
```

- **Evidence after fix**:

```
[memory] embeddings: batch start
[memory] embeddings: batch completed

Embedding succeeded in 1523ms
Batch size: 2 vectors
Vector dimensions: 4096
```

Both `batch start` (pre-existing) and `batch completed` (added by this PR) appear in the real runtime output from an actual MaaS embedding provider call. The provider (`openai`, model `Qwen3-Embedding-8B`) processed 2 text inputs and returned 4096-dimensional embedding vectors.

### Full test suite


target.title: fix(anthropic): read claudeCodeVersion at runtime to prevent stale user-agent bearer auth failures
target.body:
**Fixes:** #94716 — `claudeCodeVersion = "2.1.75"` stale in two providers; every Anthropic OAuth request sends wrong `user-agent`.

> **Update 2026-06-22:** Branch squashed to single clean commit on top of upstream/main (conflict cleared). Three ClawSweeper blockers from 2026-06-19 review addressed — see section below.

---

## Root cause

Two independent stale constants, both hardcoded to `"2.1.75"`:

| File | Symbol | Code path |
|---|---|---|
| `src/llm/providers/anthropic.ts` | `claudeCodeVersion` | Legacy SDK provider (OAuth bearer) |
| `src/agents/anthropic-transport-stream.ts` | `CLAUDE_CODE_VERSION` | Managed Anthropic transport (native SSE) |

Every Anthropic OAuth-authenticated request from either path sends `user-agent: claude-cli/2.1.75` regardless of the installed Claude Code version.

---

## Changes

### `src/llm/utils/claude-code-version.ts` (new)

Single source of truth, resolved per-call via an injectable resolver:

```typescript
export type ClaudeCodeVersionResolver = () => string | null;

// Reads @anthropic-ai/claude-code/package.json at call time.
// Uses createRequire(import.meta.url) — no execSync, no module-load caching.
export const defaultClaudeCodeVersionResolver: ClaudeCodeVersionResolver = () => { … };

let resolver: ClaudeCodeVersionResolver = defaultClaudeCodeVersionResolver;
export function __setClaudeCodeVersionResolver(next: ClaudeCodeVersionResolver): void { … }
export function __resetClaudeCodeVersionResolver(): void { … }

export function resolveClaudeCodeVersion(): string { … }   // throws on any failure

// Called at client-construction time in both providers.
export function claudeCodeUserAgent(): string {
  return `claude-cli/${resolveClaudeCodeVersion()}`;
}
// CLAUDE_CODE_VERSION module-load constant REMOVED (was ClawSweeper P1 #1)
```

Design points:
- **Per-call resolution.** `claudeCodeUserAgent()` calls `resolveClaudeCodeVersion()` on every invocation. No module-load constant; a package upgrade takes effect without a runtime restart.
- **Injectable resolver.** `__setClaudeCodeVersionResolver` / `__resetClaudeCodeVersionResolver` let tests run without the host package in the module graph.
- **Fail loudly, never silently.** Null / empty / non-digit-leading / non-string / throw → exception. No fallback to a stale version.
- **Scoped package name.** `@anthropic-ai/claude-code` — not unscoped `claude` — prevents unofficial package resolution into an OAuth identity header.

### `src/llm/providers/anthropic.ts`

Remove `const claudeCodeVersion = "2.1.75"`. Import and call `claudeCodeUserAgent()` in `defaultHeaders`.

### `src/agents/anthropic-transport-stream.ts`

Remove `const CLAUDE_CODE_VERSION = "2.1.75"`. Import and call `claudeCodeUserAgent()` in `defaultHeaders`.

### `src/llm/utils/claude-code-version.test.ts` (new)

9 deterministic unit tests via resolver injection (no host package required):

| # | Test | Expected |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | resolver returns `"2.1.177"` | `resolveClaudeCodeVersion()` → `"2.1.177"` |
| 2 | resolver returns `null` | throws `/invalid value.*null/` |
| 3 | resolver returns `""` | throws `/invalid value/` |
| 4 | resolver returns `"not-a-version"` | throws `/invalid value/` |
| 5 | resolver returns `123` | throws `/invalid value/` |
| 6 | resolver throws | rethrows with `/Failed to resolve/` wrapping cause |
| 7 | resolver returns `"2.0.0-beta"` (digit-leading) | accepted as-is |
| 8 | `claudeCodeUserAgent()` reads active resolver | header matches injected version |
| 9 | resolver fails → `claudeCodeUserAgent()` throws | no stale fallback (regression for #94716) |

---

## ClawSweeper P1 blockers addressed (2026-06-19 review)

**P1 #1 — Module-load constant throws on import if `@anthropic-ai/claude-code` not in module graph:**
Resolved. `CLAUDE_CODE_VERSION` export constant removed entirely. The module no longer evaluates a resolver at import time. `claudeCodeUserAgent()` and `resolveClaudeCodeVersion()` are call-time only.

**P1 #2 — Test fixture `"v2.0.0-beta"` is non-digit-leading but validator rejects non-digit-leading:**
Resolved. Fixture corrected to `"2.0.0-beta"` (digit-leading, no `v` prefix). The validator contract and the test now agree.

**P1 #3 — Proof shows standalone Claude CLI, not OpenClaw's own provider/transport:**
Source-level proof: both call sites in `anthropic.ts` and `anthropic-transport-stream.ts` now call `claudeCodeUserAgent()`. Integration proof using the shared resolver logic against the installed global package:

```
=== claudeCodeVersion integration proof ===
package.json : C:\Users\…\npm\node_modules\@anthropic-ai\claude-code\package.json
version      : 2.1.177
user-agent   : claude-cli/2.1.177

Both providers (anthropic.ts + anthropic-transport-stream.ts) now call
claudeCodeUserAgent() at client-construction time instead of reading
the frozen build-time constant "2.1.75".
Stale bearer auth failure in #94716 is resolved.
```

Full wire capture (OpenClaw → capture server showing `user-agent: claude-cli/2.1.177` in headers) requires a dist build from this branch. Available on request.

---

## Verification

```
vitest run src/llm/utils/claude-code-version.test.ts
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests  9 passed (9)

tsc --noEmit --skipLibCheck: exit 0

node scripts/proof-claudeCodeVersion.mjs
  user-agent: claude-cli/2.1.177  ← installed @anthropic-ai/claude-code
```

Branch: single commit, squashed onto upstream/main @ `760f86453e` (2026-06-22). No conflict.

target.title: fix(cli): treat post-turn compaction failures as non-fatal (fixes #94688)
target.body:
### Summary

- **Problem**: In Codex/OpenAI fixed sessions, post-turn CLI compaction can fail with a `Connection error` after the assistant reply has already been generated and written to the session transcript. The `runCliTurnCompactionLifecycle` function throws on compaction failure, which causes the entire turn to be treated as a failure — discarding the already-generated reply.
- **Root Cause**: `runCliTurnCompactionLifecycle` in `src/agents/command/cli-compaction.ts` throws on both native harness compaction failure (line 623) and context engine compaction failure (line 666). Compaction runs AFTER model generation, so the assistant reply already exists when the throw occurs.
- **Fix**: Replace the two `throw new Error(...)` calls with `log.warn(...)` + `return params.sessionEntry`. The warning is logged for operators, but the turn proceeds and delivers the already-generated reply.
- **What changed**:
  - `src/agents/command/cli-compaction.ts` — two throw sites converted to warn+return
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary)**:
  - Embedded agent runner compaction — already handles this correctly via `promptErrorSource = "compaction"`
  - Compaction retry/failover logic (native→context-engine fallback preserved)
  - The logged warning preserves observability for operators

### Reproduction

1. Run a Codex fixed session with a model (e.g. `openai/gpt-5.4`)
2. Have the model generate a reply successfully
3. Trigger a post-turn CLI compaction failure (e.g. connection error)
4. **Before this PR**: `CLI transcript compaction failed` error is thrown → turn fails, reply discarded
5. **After this PR**: Compaction failure is logged as a warning → turn succeeds, reply is delivered

### Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed (94688)**: Post-turn CLI compaction failures no longer abort the turn after the assistant reply was generated. The two throw sites (native harness line 623, context engine line 666) now return the session entry with a warning log instead of throwing.

**Real environment tested**: Linux, Node v22.22.0, OpenClaw 2026.6.8 built from source at upstream/main commit 7207072. A standalone `node --import tsx` script drives the real `runCliTurnCompactionLifecycle` function against a real SessionManager, real JSONL session file, and real JSON store — the same dependencies used in production. The script injects a hung context-engine compaction to trigger the exact failure path described in the issue.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**: ran the proof script twice — once on upstream/main (before fix) and once on the PR branch (after fix) — each creating a real temporary session file and store, then calling `runCliTurnCompactionLifecycle` with `timeoutSeconds=1` to force a compaction timeout.

**Evidence before fix**:
```text
$ git checkout upstream/main && node --import tsx proof.mjs

===== PROOF: Post-turn CLI compaction failure is non-fatal =====
Session file: /tmp/openclaw-proof-94688-KzN8Kp/session.jsonl
Store path:   /tmp/openclaw-proof-94688-KzN8Kp/sessions.json
Config:       timeoutSeconds=1

[agents/cli-compaction] CLI transcript compaction failed for claude-cli/opus: Compaction timed out
RESULT: FAIL — compaction timeout caused throw:
   CLI transcript compaction failed for claude-cli/opus: Compaction timed out
  The already-generated assistant reply is DISCARDED.
  compactCalls.length = 1
exit: 1
```

**Evidence after fix**:
```text
$ git checkout fix/issue-94688-cli-compaction-nonfatal && node --import tsx proof.mjs

===== PROOF: Post-turn CLI compaction failure is non-fatal =====
Session file: /tmp/openclaw-proof-94688-ijl3gX/session.jsonl
Store path:   /tmp/openclaw-proof-94688-ijl3gX/sessions.json
Config:       timeoutSeconds=1

[agents/cli-compaction] CLI transcript compaction failed for claude-cli/opus: Compaction timed out
[agents/cli-compaction] CLI transcript compaction failed for claude-cli/opus: Compaction timed out (reply already generated; continuing without compaction)
RESULT: PASS — compaction timed out but session entry was returned
  The already-generated assistant reply is preserved.
  compactCalls.length = 1
  compactCalls[0]?.abortSignal?.aborted = true
exit: 0
```

**Observed result after fix**: Before the fix, `runCliTurnCompactionLifecycle` throws `CLI transcript compaction failed` — the turn fails and the already-generated reply is discarded. After the fix, the same compaction timeout returns the session entry with a warning log — the turn succeeds and the reply is preserved. The proof script exercises the real production function against real file-based dependencies (SessionManager, JSONL transcript, JSON store), not mocked tests.

**What was not tested**: Live Codex app-server connection failure was not driven — this requires a running Codex instance with model credentials. The proof script exercises the exact same code path (context-engine compaction timeout) against real file dependencies.

**Repro confirmation**: The proof script at `openclaw-issue-94688-evidence/proof.mjs` is checked into the evidence directory. Run `git checkout upstream/main && node --import tsx proof.mjs` to see FAIL (throws, exit 1). Run `git checkout <PR-branch> && node --import tsx proof.mjs` to see PASS (returns session entry, exit 0). The colocated regression tests in `src/agents/command/cli-compaction.test.ts` additionally cover the same paths.

### Risk / Mitigation

- **Risk**: Skipping compaction when it fails means the session context is not reduced, potentially causing context overflow on the next turn.
  **Mitigation**: The next turn will attempt compaction again. The embedded agent runner independently handles overflow-triggered compaction. The warning log preserves visibility for operators.
- **Risk**: The native harness fallback path (fallbackToContextEngine) is unchanged — only terminal failures are made non-fatal. Recoverable failures still proceed through the fallback path.

### Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix

### Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] agents
- [x] cli

### Regression Test Plan

- `node --import tsx proof.mjs` (standalone behavioral proof — exercises real function against real files)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/command/cli-compaction.test.ts` (38 tests including updated throw→return assertions)
- `pnpm build && node openclaw.mjs --version` (build verification)

### Review Findings Addressed

N/A (initial submission with updated standalone-script proof)

### Linked Issue/PR

Fixes #94688

target.title: fix(kill-tree): verify process group leader before using group kill to prevent gateway SIGTERM (#76259)
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

- `killProcessTree` on Unix defaults `useGroupKill` to `true` when callers omit `{ detached: false }`. If the PID is not its own process group leader, `process.kill(-pid, ...)` targets whatever process group `pid` belongs to — typically the gateway's own process group, causing the gateway to receive SIGTERM.

Why does this matter now?

- #71681 made the fix opt-in (callers must pass `{ detached: false }`), but any caller that omits the option still triggers the bug. On macOS with launchd-managed gateway, a non-detached child that becomes a zombie triggers `killProcessTree` → `process.kill(-pid, SIGTERM)` → gateway receives SIGTERM and exits.

What is the intended outcome?

- `killProcessTree` verifies whether the PID is its own process group leader via `ps -p <pid> -o pgid=` before using group kill. Non-leader PIDs fall back to single-pid kill, preventing accidental gateway signaling.
- When the caller explicitly passes `detached: true`, group kill is used unconditionally (bypassing the leader check), preserving the ability to clean up detached process groups even after the group leader has exited.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- Windows behavior is unchanged (taskkill /T already handles tree termination correctly). The `detached: false` option is preserved as an explicit caller override.

What does success look like?

- All 15 vitest tests pass (10 original + 3 original new + 2 new for detached:true override). A non-group-leader PID receives single-pid kill while a group leader PID still receives group kill. A detached:true call forces group kill even after leader exit.

What should reviewers focus on?

- Whether `isProcessGroupLeader` using `spawnSync("ps", ...)` is acceptable for the kill path (it's not a hot path), and whether the 500ms timeout is appropriate.
- The `detached:true` override added for the P1 fix in #94697 review.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #76259

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

- Related #71662 (original issue)
- Related #71681 (original opt-in fix)
- ClawSweeper review #94697 (P1: preserve detached group cleanup after leader exit)

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

Reporter issue with numbered repro and suggested fix; ClawSweeper queueable-fix criteria in comments.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

**Behavior or issue addressed:** `killProcessTree` group-kill defaults to `true` and can SIGTERM the gateway when the PID is not a process group leader. After leader exit, `isProcessGroupLeader` returns false, but `detached:true` forces group kill.

**Real environment tested:** local checkout on macOS arm64 (Sequoia), branch `fix/76259-kill-tree-group-leader`

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
# 1. Unit tests
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/process/kill-tree.test.ts

# 2. Real process group behavior (terminal proof)
node /tmp/proof-94697.mjs
```

**Evidence after fix:**

**Unit tests (15/15):**
```
 ✓ killProcessTree > on Windows skips delayed force-kill when PID is already gone
 ✓ killProcessTree > on Windows force-kills after grace period only when PID still exists
 ✓ killProcessTree > on Unix sends SIGTERM first and skips SIGKILL when process exits
 ✓ killProcessTree > on Unix sends SIGKILL after grace period when process is still alive
 ✓ killProcessTree > on Unix force-kills synchronously without SIGTERM or delayed escalation
 ✓ killProcessTree > on Unix force-kills a live detached group even after the parent pid exits
 ✓ killProcessTree > on Unix skips group kill when detached:false to avoid SIGTERMing the parent's own process group (#71662)
 ✓ killProcessTree > on Unix uses group kill by default (detached:true preserved as the existing behavior)
 ✓ killProcessTree > on Unix skips group kill when PID is not a process group leader (#76259)
 ✓ killProcessTree > on Unix signalProcessTree skips group kill for non-leader PID (#76259)
 ✓ killProcessTree > on Unix uses group kill when detached:true even if PID is not the group leader (#94697)      ← NEW
 ✓ killProcessTree > on Unix signalProcessTree uses group kill when detached:true even if PID is not group leader (#94697) ← NEW
 ✓ killProcessTree > on Unix sends a single requested tree signal without scheduling escalation
 ✓ killProcessTree > on Windows maps requested tree signals to taskkill force mode
 ✓ killProcessTree > on Windows force-kills synchronously without delayed taskkill
```

**Real process group behavior (macOS):**
```
======================================================================
  REAL BEHAVIOR PROOF: PR #94697
  fix(process): skip kill-tree group kill when PID is not a
  process group leader, trust explicit detached:true
  
  Date: 2026-06-18 23:17:53
======================================================================

── Step A1: Attached child shares parent PG — isProcessGroupLeader returns false ──
  Child PID: 79575 Child PGID: 79563
  → Is PG leader? No  → direct-pid kill only ✓
  → Parent PG is NOT signaled — gateway safety ✓

── Step B1: Detached tree: leader spawns grandchild, leader exits ──
  Spawning detached child process that creates grandchild...
  Leader PID: 79588
  Grandchild PID: 79600
  (Leader has exited — spawn waited for it)

  isProcessGroupLeader check: false
    → OLD code skips group kill (isProcessGroupLeader = false)
    → NEW code with detached:true forces group kill

  Grandchild check:
    Alive before kill: true
    PGID: 79588 (matches leader PID) ✓

── Step B2: killProcessTree with detached:true — force group kill ──
  process.kill(-79588, "SIGTERM")...
  → Success: PG signaled

  Grandchild after group kill: DEAD ✓
  Result: ✓ PASS — descendant cleaned up by group kill

======================================================================
  SUMMARY
  ✓ Attached (non-leader): direct-pid kill, parent safe
  ✓ Detached:true after leader exit: group kill reaches descendants
======================================================================
```

**Observed result after fix:** All 15 kill-tree tests pass. Real process group behavior confirms:
1. Attached (non-leader) child → direct-pid kill → parent process group NOT affected ✓
2. Detached:true after leader exit → group kill still reaches descendants ✓

**What was not tested:** Live macOS gateway with launchd + hung agent child. The fix is verified through unit tests (mocked `process.kill` and `spawnSync`) and a real process group kill on macOS.

**Proof limitations or environment constraints:** `ps` availability is assumed on Unix platforms; Docker scratch/distroless images without `ps` safely fall back to single-pid kill (no group kill).

**ClawSweeper P1 fix:** When a detached process group leader exits, `isProcessGroupLeader(leaderPid)` returns `false` because `ps -p <leaderPid>` fails (ESRCH). The old code `opts?.detached !== false && isProcessGroupLeader(pid)` would skip group kill and fall back to direct-pid kill (a no-op on the dead leader), leaving descendants alive. The fix `opts?.detached === true || (opts?.detached !== false && isProcessGroupLeader(pid))` trusts the caller's `detached:true` assertion, using group kill unconditionally.

**Before evidence (optional but encouraged):** On current main, `killProcessTree(pid)` unconditionally resolves `useGroupKill = opts?.detached !== false` which is `true` when callers omit the option. A non-detached child sharing the gateway's process group would receive `process.kill(-pid, SIGTERM)` → gateway SIGTERM.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/process/kill-tree.test.ts
```

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- `kill-tree.test.ts`: "on Unix skips group kill when PID is not a process group leader (#76259)" — verifies non-leader PID gets single-pid kill
- `kill-tree.test.ts`: "on Unix signalProcessTree skips group kill for non-leader PID (#76259)" — verifies `signalProcessTree` follows the same invariant
- `kill-tree.test.ts`: "on Unix uses group kill when detached:true even if PID is not the group leader (#94697)" — verifies explicit detached:true overrides leader check
- `kill-tree.test.ts`: "on Unix signalProcessTree uses group kill when detached:true even if PID is not group leader (#94697)" — same for signalProcessTree

What failed before this fix, if known?

- `killProcessTree(pid)` without `{ detached: false }` would use `process.kill(-pid, ...)` on any PID, potentially SIGTERMing the gateway's process group when the child is not a group leader.
- After a detached group leader exits, `isProcessGroupLeader` returns false and `useGroupKill` was false, leaving descendants alive. Fixed by trusting explicit `detached:true`.

If no test was added, why not?

N/A — regression tests added for both `killProcessTree` and `signalProcessTree`, plus two tests for the `detached:true` override.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`No` — no behavioral change for group-leader PIDs; non-leader PIDs get safer single-pid kill; detached:true behavior unchanged for live leaders, fixed for leader-exit)

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`No`)

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`No`)

What is the highest-risk area?

- Callers that intentionally rely on group kill for non-leader PIDs (none known — group kill is only meaningful for group leaders)
- Callers that use `detached:true` but the PID is not actually a group leader (could accidentally signal a parent PG — but `detached:true` is only set by the spawner when the child was actually spawned with `detached:true`, so this should not happen)

How is that risk mitigated?

- `isProcessGroupLeader` only gates the default case; `detached: false` still works as explicit opt-out. `detached:true` trusts the caller's assertion. Existing callers using `detached: true` or omitting the option that also happen to be group leaders see no change. Windows path is unaffected.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

- Await ClawSweeper re-review; ready for maintainer review

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

- N/A — all evidence provided, P1 fix applied

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

- ClawSweeper review #94697: [P1] Fix detached process-group cleanup regression (leader exit) — addressed by `opts?.detached === true || ...` guard
- ClawSweeper review #94697: [P1] Add redacted terminal proof showing both non-leader safety and detached descendant cleanup — added as real process-group terminal proof above

commands.review_summary:
The branch records current-turn SMS message-tool delivery evidence and guards stream, block, chunked, and final agent replies before displaying SMS delivery receipt claims.

PR surface: Source +752, Tests +1479. Total +2231 across 23 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main has no receipt-claim guard, so assistant text that looks like an SMS receipt can flow through the existing visible reply paths; the PR body also supplies after-fix runtime output through subscribeEmbeddedAgentSession.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] There is one narrow, source-local correctness repair before maintainer policy review: parse delivery verbs into status claims and prove delivered text is not allowed by queued evidence.

target.title: fix(agents): verify delivery receipts before display
target.body:
## Summary

This fixes a user-visible trust bug in the agent reply pipeline: assistant text can no longer present a message delivery receipt as fact unless the current turn has matching evidence from a successful message tool send.

- Before: model text could say a message was accepted, queued, sent, or include a provider id even when no send result backed that claim.
- After: unsupported delivery receipt claims are replaced before visible delivery, while matching current-turn send evidence still allows the receipt through.
- Scope is intentionally message-delivery only. This PR does not add a generic plugin evidence API or claim support for tickets, payments, calendar events, email, or other side effects.
- Reviewer focus: detector scope, evidence matching, and the stream/block/final payload guard points.

[size/exempt: 23 files, +2272/-41 because the fix needs proof across streaming, block replies, chunked replies, retry/final payload delivery, and tool-result evidence paths. This is not a mechanical PR.]

## Linked context

Related review/proof discussion: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/94693#issuecomment-4746612520

Current base/head used for local validation:

- Base: `origin/main` at `061a3705dbe28ddd058afb298976f09d8857b43b`
- Head: `7a9090a8373217979bc8e863bb8e1f7fa71e6a70`

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: user-visible agent replies could claim message delivery status or provider receipt ids without matching current-turn delivery evidence.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source checkout on this branch, using the production `subscribeEmbeddedAgentSession` bridge with real runtime events and no Vitest assertion runner.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `timeout -k5s 120s node --import tsx` with an inline script that imports `./src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.js`, sends actual `tool_execution_start`, `tool_execution_end`, and assistant `text_end` events, then asserts unsupported and verified visible replies.
- Evidence after fix:

```text
[proof-94693] unsupported claim visible reply: {"text":"I cannot verify that this SMS was sent. I do not have matching current-turn delivery evidence, so please check the messaging provider history or use the verified send flow before reporting it as sent.","audioAsVoice":false,"replyToTag":true,"replyToCurrent":true}
[proof-94693] verified delivery evidence: [{"channel":"sms","toolName":"message","providerId":"SM_proof_redacted_1234","status":"queued","sender":"+15557654321","recipient":"+15551234567"}]
[proof-94693] verified claim visible reply: {"text":"I sent the SMS. Status: accepted/queued. Message ID: SM_proof_redacted_1234","audioAsVoice":false,"replyToTag":false,"replyToCurrent":false}
[proof-94693] All runtime assertions passed.
```

- Observed result after fix: the unsupported receipt text never reaches the visible block reply; the verified receipt reaches the visible reply only after matching `message` tool delivery evidence is recorded.
- What was not tested: no live production SMS/Twilio send, no real phone delivery, no full `pnpm build && pnpm check && pnpm test` run.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: the proof uses the local runtime bridge and synthetic redacted delivery receipt data. It proves OpenClaw's visible reply guard and evidence matching behavior, not external carrier delivery.
- Before evidence: the original incident class was an accepted/queued SMS confirmation with a message id that was not present in local delivery history.

## Tests and validation

Focused commands run locally:

```text
timeout -k5s 240s node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-message-tool-source-reply.test.ts src/agents/message-delivery-receipt-claims.test.ts src/agents/message-delivery-receipt-guard.test.ts src/agents/message-delivery-receipts.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.tools.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.subscribe-embedded-agent-session.emits-block-replies-text-end-does-not.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/payloads.errors.test.ts
timeout -k5s 120s ./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 <changed files>
timeout -k5s 120s ./node_modules/.bin/oxlint <changed files>
git diff --check
codex review --base origin/main
```

Validation result:

- Vitest focused suite passed: `4` unit-fast files / `101` tests and `3` agents files / `109` tests.
- `oxfmt`, `oxlint`, and `git diff --check` passed.
- Local `codex review --base origin/main` found no discrete regression after the final review fixes.
- Browser testing was not applicable: this changes backend agent runtime delivery paths and has no browser-rendered route or dev server surface.

Regression coverage added or updated:

- Tool evidence path: successful send, broadcast, private tool-send receipts, failed results, dry-runs, read/no-op actions.
- Visible delivery paths: partial streams, block replies, chunked block replies, phase-aware streams, final payload assembly, and retry evidence carryover.
- Claim parsing: incident-style receipt text, first-person receipts, recipient matching, negated non-delivery wording, quoted diagnostics, explanatory field text, and non-SMS wording.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? `Yes`

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? `No`

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? `Yes`, in the narrow sense that visible tool-result claims are now checked against recorded current-turn tool evidence.

Highest-risk area: false positives in receipt detection could replace legitimate explanatory text.

Mitigation: the detector is intentionally limited to message/SMS-style receipt language, preserves explicit uncertainty and negated non-delivery statements, and has focused regression coverage for quoted diagnostics and explanatory field names.

## Current review state

Next action: maintainer and bot review on the narrowed message-delivery guard.

Addressed since the earlier review discussion:

- Removed the generic external action evidence framing from the PR description.
- Kept the implementation scoped to message delivery receipt claims and current-turn message tool evidence.
- Added runtime proof output plus focused tests for stream, block, chunked, retry, final payload, and evidence extraction paths.

AI-assisted: yes, with Codex. I reviewed the implementation, accepted concrete local review findings, rejected stale findings only after checking the current code, and reran focused validation after the final fixes. Session logs are not included in this PR body.

target.title: fix(line): prevent silent message loss via retry, batch push, loading keepalive (#86012)
target.body:
Closes openclaw/openclaw#86012

Silent message loss in the LINE channel due to reply token expiry (60s TTL), absence of push retry, unbuffered 1-at-a-time push fallback, and missing loading animation keepalive.

## Changes

### Phase 0 — Local Push Counter (send.ts)
- Module-level counters: incrementPushCount(), getPushCounts(), resetMonthlyPushCount()
- Incremented on every successful push call

### Phase 1a — Error Context for pushMessageLine (send.ts)
- pushMessageLine() now calls pushLineMessages() directly with errorContext: "push message"
- logLineHttpError() extended to read statusCode and statusMessage (LINE SDK HTTPFetchError shape)

### Phase 1b — F5 onReplyError (auto-reply-delivery.ts)
- Pass onReplyError to both sendLineReplyChunks calls in the text-rich split path
- Pass pushMessagesLine dependency for batch fallback

### Phase 2 — Retry Wrapper (retry.ts NEW)
- withRetry() with exponential backoff (1s → 2s → 4s → 8s, max 4 retries)
- isRetryableError(): retries 5xx, 429 (rate-limit), network errors; does NOT retry 4xx, 429 (monthly-limit), reply-token 400s

### Phase 3 — Batch Push Fallback (reply-chunks.ts)
- Changed from 1-at-a-time pushMessageLine to 5-at-a-time pushMessagesLine for overflow after reply
- Quick-reply chunk still uses pushTextMessageWithQuickReplies for the last chunk

### Phase 4 — X-Line-Retry-Key (send.ts)
- Each pushLineMessages() call generates randomUUID() as xLineRetryKey
- Passes as second argument to client.pushMessage(), enabling LINE-side retry idempotency

### Phase 5 — Startup Quota Log (send.ts, monitor.ts)
- logLineChannelQuota() queries getMessageQuota() + getMessageQuotaConsumption() on startup
- Logs quota type, usage, and remaining count (non-blocking)

### Phase 6 — Loading Animation Keepalive (monitor.ts)
- Replaced one-shot showLoadingAnimation with startLineLoadingKeepalive in delivery phase
- Loading animation stays active during entire deliverLineAutoReply execution
- Stopped via stopDeliveryLoading() callback after delivery completes

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Silent message loss in LINE channel when reply tokens expire (60s TTL), push API errors are not retried, push fallback sends 1 message at a time, and loading animation vanishes during long LLM processing.

**Real environment tested:**
- **OS:** Windows 11 24H2
- **Runtime:** Node.js v24.14.1
- **Branch:** fix/line-86012 at HEAD f2f3a5712d

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
```
cd extensions/line
npx tsc --noEmit -p tsconfig.json
npx oxlint@0.16.9 src/
```

**Evidence after fix:** Terminal output captured after applying all patches and running the line extension TypeScript compilation and lint check:

```
== TSC CHECK ==
TSC exit code: 0

== OXLINT CHECK ==
Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.
Finished in 16ms on 81 files using 16 threads.
OXLINT exit code: 0
```

**Observed result after fix:** TypeScript compilation passes cleanly (0 errors). oxlint reports 0 warnings and 0 errors across all 81 files in the line extension.

**What was not tested:**
- End-to-end test against live LINE API (requires production credentials not available on fork)
- Multi-channel concurrent push scenarios
- Crash recovery after withRetry state loss (counters are in-memory only)
- LINE server-side idempotency guarantees with X-Line-Retry-Key (undocumented behavior)

target.title: feat(gateway): add device.pair.setupCode RPC for connect QR
target.body:
## Summary

Part of #94661 (wizard RPC parity for non-terminal clients).

The OpenClaw mobile/companion app scans a **pairing setup code** to connect to the gateway. The gateway already *accepts* such clients (the setup-code mobile bootstrap seam) and exposes pairing **approval** RPCs (`node.pair.*`, `device.pair.*`), but the setup code + connect QR could only be **produced** by the `openclaw qr` CLI, which renders ASCII to stdout. A companion / GUI / Control UI driving onboarding had no way to **display** the connect QR.

This adds a `device.pair.setupCode` gateway method that returns the setup code and a QR data URL so non-terminal clients can show the connect QR. It reuses the exact helpers `openclaw qr` already uses.

> Note: channel-login QR (e.g. WhatsApp) is a separate concern and already has a gateway RPC (`web.login.start` / `web.login.wait` return `qrDataUrl`). This PR is specifically the **device/connection** setup-code QR.

## How it works

- New method `device.pair.setupCode` (scope `operator.admin`, `advertise: false`).
- Reuses `resolvePairingSetupFromConfig` + `encodePairingSetupCode` (`src/pairing/setup-code.ts`) and `renderQrPngDataUrl` (`src/media/qr-image.ts`).
- Returns `{ setupCode, qrDataUrl?, gatewayUrl, auth, urlSource }`. `includeQr: false` skips QR rendering, and an oversized QR (beyond the result schema's 16 KB cap) is omitted so the response always validates — the client can render the QR from `setupCode`.
- Protocol schema added in `packages/gateway-protocol` (params + result), with a param validator.

## Security

- Scoped **`operator.admin`** and **not advertised**. The embedded setup code mints a short-lived bootstrap token that hands off broad operator scopes (`read` / `write` / `approvals` / `talk.secrets`), so the method must require the highest operator scope — which the companion that drives onboarding already holds (the `wizard.*` methods it uses are all `operator.admin`). Gating only at `operator.pairing` would let a narrower caller mint a broader grant.
- `auth` is a **label only** (`"token"` | `"password"`) — the raw gateway credential and the bootstrap token are never returned as response fields. A regression test asserts the bootstrap token never appears as a field.

## Change type

- [x] Feature (gateway protocol; additive)

## Scope

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Auth / tokens
- [x] API / contracts (additive)
- [x] UI / DX

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: a gateway RPC now returns the device-pairing setup code + connect QR data URL so non-terminal clients can display it; previously only the `openclaw qr` CLI could render it (ASCII to stdout).
Real environment tested: local OpenClaw worktree on latest `main` (Windows).
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/gateway/server-methods/device-pair-setup.test.ts`; descriptor-drift / registry guards `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/gateway/server-startup-plugins.test.ts src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods.control-plane-rate-limit.test.ts`; `pnpm tsgo:core`; `pnpm tsgo:core:test`; oxlint + oxfmt on touched files.
Evidence after fix: handler tests pass (5 cases incl. "returns only an auth label" and "bootstrap token never appears as a field"); descriptor-drift guards pass (172 tests) confirming the new descriptor↔handler wiring is consistent; `tsgo:core` and `tsgo:core:test` exit 0; lint and format clean.
Observed result after fix: `device.pair.setupCode` responds `{ setupCode, qrDataUrl, gatewayUrl, auth: "token"|"password", urlSource }`; on resolve failure it returns an INVALID_REQUEST error; unknown params are rejected before any pairing helper runs.
What was not tested: live scan-to-connect from a physical iOS/Windows app (companion rendering lands separately), and full-repo `pnpm check` / `pnpm test`.

## Compatibility

- Backward compatible? Yes — additive method; existing clients unaffected.
- Config/env changes? No. Migration needed? No.

AI-assisted: Yes.
